# Extracao: transcription_mp4_4__Commit_to_the_Steps

You're like, man, when are we going to get to the steps? I mean, you've got to look. Let's get commitments in first. OK? You've got to make some commitments so that this thing works. If you don't make commitments, nothing's going to work. The first commitment I want you to make to these steps is this. I want you to know the steps by heart. You know, attitude, greeting, fact finding, appraisal, selection, demonstration, trial close, write up, negotiate, close, delivery, and follow up. Do you know them? Do you know them backwards? How about this? Follow up, delivery, close, negotiate, write up, trial close, demonstration, selection, appraisal, fact finding, greeting, and attitude. See, you want to know them backwards and forward. You want to know them like, oh, wow, man, I just missed step three. I'm in step five. I'm going to go back and pick up step three. You want to know them so that you get them all covered, so you're fluid, not mechanical. Number two, commitment. You've got to know what the purpose of each step is. And you have to know the benefits to the customer first. All right? Look, follow me. I want to show you something. It's going to save us time and money. You see, I'm giving them a benefit. I'm moving to steps. I know why they benefit people. I know why the demonstration is important. Management's not having to tell me, go back and do that. You have to know this for yourself now. Number three, I want you never, ever. I want you to make a commitment. I am never, ever going to skip a step. I'm going to know every step. And I'm going to discipline myself to make sure that I do each step that my company wants me to do. That's a commitment you need to make. I'm committed to every step. I commit to the steps. And I commit to doing them with every customer, every time, every day, regardless of the situation. Number four, always get answers to questions. Sir, are you going to be paying for this one time or over a period of time? No answer. Or I don't know. By the way, I don't know is no answer. You know how many people give you an I don't know answer? That is no answer. Look, ask until you get an answer. Sir, are you going to be making these payments one time or over a period of time? You know, I'm not sure yet. What would you prefer doing? I mean, if you were going to make a decision right now. You know, I really don't know yet. I understand you don't know. How'd you do it last time? Oh, yeah, we made payments the last time. And at the time before that, we made payments. And at the time before that, we made payments. What do you think he's going to do, man? See, you got an answer to the question. OK, the fifth commitment, always handle customers' originations. Oh, the weather is terrible today. That's an origination. Always handle the customers' originations and objections. Things like, I don't need to drive it. Sir, I totally understand you don't need to drive it. You know what? I tell you what, I'll drive it. You be the passenger. OK? Hey, Grant, I don't want to dance with you. Hey, no problem. How about we have a drink instead? See, I want to handle the objection. I want to not act like it didn't happen. I want to handle the origination. The weather is terrible. Hey, I agree with you. Handle the origination. Acknowledge it. If it's an objection, handle it. OK? Number six commitment, always be positive with everything you do. Look, the amount of positive can do. No problem. That attitude is contagious. It's everything to you, your career, your happiness, and your bank account. That's why you need to be on sales training VT every day. You need to be on this daily and consult it before and after deals. Look, if you've ever lost a deal, your attitude drops. That's when you want to go get it fixed. Don't wait for three days. Fix your attitude and keep it positive. OK, commitment seven, always agree with your customer. Look, this is the first rule of selling. I introduce you to this in the fundamentals of selling. The first rule of selling must be in, always agree with the customer. Because if you don't agree with people, you can't handle all their points of resistance. If you can't handle the points of resistance, you will not control the sales process and you will not go through the steps. Commitment eight, always approach each of the steps from the mode of service. This isn't a step that I'm taking. Hey, welcome to our dealership. My name's Grant, and yours? See, that's not a step that I'm taking. I know what the benefit is. People do business with people they like. You agree with that? People do business with people they like and feel good with. Trust and are more likely to do business with me and pay me because they feel good around me. That's the benefits. You have to not just know the steps, but you need to know, hey, man, how does it benefit them? Not how does it benefit me and the company? How does it benefit them? Those are your commitments. Make them, learn them, and commit to them.
